user.title: Configure QWeather Pro
user.description:
### ⚙️ Setup Steps 
 **Go to the [QWeather Console](https://console.qweather.com):** 
 1. In Settings, copy the **API Host** and fill it into the **API Host** field below. 
 2. Project Management → Create a project, then add credentials to the project: optional **JSON Web Token** and **API KEY**.
 3. For the location, it is recommended to enter latitude and longitude (Gaode coordinates) or a city name. Optional: [City ID](https://github.com/qwd/LocationList/blob/master/China-City-List-latest.csv). 
 The default location is the HA latitude and longitude. 
 #### Note: **API KEY** authentication does not provide sufficient security. All requests using **API KEY** authentication are subject to rate limits. It is recommended to use **JSON Web Token (JWT)** authentication!

data.location_id: Geographical location. Supports city name, city ID, or latitude and longitude (longitude first).
data.api_key: Ordinary API Key (Leave blank if using JWT)

jwt_setup.title: JWT Security Configuration
jwt_setup.description:
The system has generated a public key for you.

1. Copy the **Public Key** below.
2. Add it to [QWeather Console](https://console.qweather.com) as a **JSON Web Token** credential.

```text
{public_key}
```

Then enter the IDs below.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to QWeather server.
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Check your Key or JWT credentials.
error.jwt_error: JWT signature failed. Please check the generated keys.
error.api_key_missing: API Key is required for Standard Mode.
error.api_host_deprecated: Public API has been deprecated. Please use the new API Host.

init.title: Integration Options
init.description:
### 💡 Usage Notes
- **Forecast Limits**: For the **Free Version**, Daily Forecast should be **3** or **7** days, and Hourly Forecast should be **24** hours.
- **Grid Weather**: When using High Precision Grid API, certain observation data (e.g., **Visibility**) will not be available and will show as `--` on the card.
